report.summary: Polished 2 new learn guides (On-Chain Analytics, Crypto Arbitrage Strategies) with UX fixes: replaced custom breadcrumbs with Breadcrumb component, added scroll-margin-top to all TOC anchor sections, fixed BackToTop positioning, normalized related-link padding, and added cross-links between the two new pages.

sections.title: UX Improvements
items: Replaced custom inline breadcrumb nav with Breadcrumb component on both new pages — adds Home link and consistent styling site-wide
Added scroll-mt-20 to all 17 anchor sections across both pages — prevents content hiding under sticky nav when clicking TOC links
Moved BackToTop outside <article> on on-chain analytics page for consistent positioning with other learn pages
Normalized related-link card padding from p-4 to p-6 on arbitrage page for visual consistency with on-chain analytics page
Added bidirectional cross-links between the two new guides in their Related Resources sections

sections.title: Visuals Created
items: OG image already exists: og-onchain-analytics-guide-2026.svg (1200x630) — features on-chain data chart with whale alert dots, volume bars, and smart money annotations
OG image already exists: og-crypto-arbitrage-strategies-guide-2026.svg (1200x630) — features CEX/DEX price spread visualization with profit accumulation chart and strategy pills
No new OG images needed — both SVGs follow brand guidelines (dark background, accent gradient bar, degen0x logo, decorative elements)

sections.title: UX Audit Notes
items: Both pages have good progressive disclosure: intro → TOC → detailed sections → FAQ → related links
Table of Contents with anchor links follows standard learn-page pattern
Color-coded badges (Analytics/Intermediate, Trading/Advanced) correctly indicate content level
Glass-card info boxes used effectively for key concepts (Exchange Inflow/Outflow, Arbitrage Sources)
Comparison table in arbitrage guide has overflow-x-auto for mobile responsiveness
Pre-existing TypeScript JSX escape issues on arbitrage page (lines 410, 705, 825, 859) — unrelated to design changes, project uses ignoreBuildErrors
